header.roll.you: You can <roll>roll the dice</roll> or <add>take an extra dice</add>
header.reroll.you: {rollLeft, plural, one{# remaining roll} other{# remaining rolls}}: <roll>reroll {dice, plural, one{# die} other{# dice}}</roll> or <stop>keep these dice</stop>
header.roll.player: {player} rolls the dice ({rollLeft, plural, one{# remaining roll} other{# remaining rolls}})
header.buy.you: Buy a card, <renew>replace the cards</renew> or <pass>pass</pass> (+1 <energy/>)
header.renew.confirm:
You may spend 2 <energy/> to discard all 3 face-up Power cards and reveal 3 new ones, which are immediately available
to buy.
header.buy-more.you: Buy another card, <renew>replace the cards</renew> or <pass>pass</pass>
header.buzz.you: Place the Buzz token on the board
header.buzz.player: {player} places the Buzz token on the board
header.titanic-batteries.you: Titanic batteries: select 2 dice to ignore or <pass>pass</pass>
header.titanic-batteries.player: Titanic batteries: {player} can ignore 2 dice to win <token/>
header.superconductor.player: Superconductor: {player} can discard the card to win {count} <energy/>
header.camouflage.you: Camouflage: you roll {count} dice to prevent up to {count} <hit/>
header.camouflage.player: Camouflage: {player} rolls {count} dice to prevent up to {count} <hit/>
header.dominate.you: Dominate: discard Power cards or <pass>roll {count, plural, one{# less die} other{# less dice}}</pass>
header.dominate.player: Dominate: {player} can discard Power cards before rolling the dice
header.hibernation.you: <discard>discard Hibernation</discard> or <pass>skip your turn</pass> to gain 6 <heart/>
header.hibernation.player: {player} discards Hibernation or skips turn to earn 6 <heart/>
header.made-in-a-lab.you: Made in a lab: buy a card for -1 <energy/> or <pass>pass</pass>
header.made-in-a-lab.player: Made in a lab: {player} can buy a card for -1<energy/>
header.rebooting.player: {player} chooses whether to use Rebooting or resolve the dice
header.tesla-impulse.you: Tesla impulse: place a Power card from {rival} in the discard pile
header.tesla-impulse.player: Tesla impulse: {player} discards one of your Power cards
header.tesla-impulse.spectator: Tesla impulse: {player} discards a Power card from {rival}
header.unstable-dna.you: Unstable DNA: take a Power card from {rival} or <pass>pass</pass>
header.unstable-dna.player: Unstable DNA: {player} can take a Power card from you
header.unstable-dna.spectator: Unstable DNA: {player} can take a Power card from {rival}
header.in-shape.player: In shape: {player} can pay 1 <energy/> to pull 1 <fame/> (x{count})
monster.power.1:
<power/> <power/> > Add any symbol to your Roll.
+ <power/> > Add this symbol again.
Alienoid can use groups of 2 or 3 <power/> symbols as any die symbol.
monster.power.2:
<power/> <power/> > +2 <energy/>
+ <power/> > +2 <energy/>
Cyber Kitty can use combos of <power/> to gain a lot of <energy/>.
monster.power.3:
<power/> <power/> > Pull 1 <fame/> or 1 <destruction/>,
Gigazaur can pull either the <fame/> or <destruction/> with 2 <power/> symbols.
monster.power.4:
You can treat <smash/> in your Roll as <smash/> x <power/>.
Meka Dragon can multiply the total number of <smash/> by the number of <power/> when resolving your Roll.
monster.power.5:
<power/> > +1<dice/>
Space Penguin can generate <dice/> to roll more dice on future turns.
monster.power.6:
<power/> <power/> > <kingbuzz/>.
The King can place Buzz tokens that slow his Rival’s progress on the tracks.
dice.help: Each die has 6 symbols that represent the actions you can take on your turn.
dice.smash: <smashFace/> <bold>Smash: Your Rival takes 1</bold> <hit/> <bold>for each</bold> <smashFace/> <bold>rolled.</bold> Each <hit/> causes your Rival to lose 1 <heart/>. If they lose their last <heart/>, the <death/> symbol appears and you win the game.
dice.hearth:
<heartFace/> <bold>Heal: Gain 1</bold><heart/> <bold>for each</bold> <heartFace/> <bold>rolled.</bold>
You cannot gain more than your starting <heart/>.
dice.energy:
<energyFace/> <bold>Energy: Gain 1</bold><energy/> <bold>from the reserve for each</bold> <energyFace/> <bold>rolled and place it in front of you.</bold>
The <energy/> represent Energy cubes. Keep them in front of you until you spend them.
dice.pull:
<fameFace/> <bold>Fame and</bold> <destructionFace/> <bold>Destruction: For each triple</bold> <fameFace/> <bold>or</bold> <destructionFace/> <bold>rolled, pull the corresponding</bold> <fame/> <bold>or</bold> <destruction/> <bold>marker 1 space towards you on its track.</bold>
<bold>For each additional matching symbol rolled, pull the corresponding</bold> <fame/> <bold>or</bold> <destruction/> <bold>marker 1 additional space towards you.</bold>
If you pull the <fame/> or <destruction/>marker onto the Victory space on your side of the board, or if both markers are in the Spotlight Zones on your side of the board, you immediately win the game.
dice.power:
<powerFace/> <bold>Special Power: Check the special power on your Monster board.</bold>
If you have enough <powerFace/> in your Roll, you may activate your special power multiple times.
Any "+ <powerFace/> >" effects on your Monster board may only be used once each time you activate your special power.
dice.token.help:
Before your first roll, you may discard Dice tokens <dice/> to roll an equal number
of extra dice during this phase.

These tokens and dice are obtained using some Power cards or Monster powers.
Each player may possess a maximum of 2.
buzz.token.help:
Buzz tokens have 1 to 3 spaces, most of which contain a Buzz bonus.

To place this token on the board, you need to buy an associated Power card.

When a player moves his Fame or Destruction token onto a Buzz bonus, he immediately wins the bonus.
buzz.king:
Those tokens can only be placed on the board or moved by The King, using his special power.

When the King's Rival pulls a marker onto this Buzz token, its movement ends immediately, and any unused movement on the track is lost.
pawn.help:
If a player manages to draw the Glory or Destruction marker to the end of the track, he wins.
Similarly, he wins the game if he manages to draw the 2 markers into the "Spotlight Zone" on his side of the board.
energy.help: Energy cubes are used to buy Power cards.
board.help:
On the board, Fame and Destruction markers start in the middle of their respective tracks.

On these tracks, each player has a "spotlight" area.

Pulling the 2 marker in their "under the spotlight" area wins the game.

Similarly, pulling one of the two counters to the end of the track wins the game.
deck.help: There {number, plural, one{is # card} other{are # cards}} in the draw pile.
card.cost.discount: At this location, the card costs 1<energy/> less.
card.buzz: This card has a buzz icon below its cost. When you buy it, you can place, or move, the corresponding buzz token on the board.
card.keep: Keep: after purchasing this card, you keep it in your playing area.
card.discard: Discard: after purchasing this card, apply the effect immediately then discard it.
card.effect.2: <bold>Add 1</bold><smashFace/> to your Roll for each <destruction/> and <fame/> marker in a <italic>Spotlight Zone</italic>.
card.effect.3: Once per turn, <bold>you may discard a Power card from the board</bold>.
card.effect.4: If you have at least 5 of a kind in your Roll, <bold>your Rival takes 4</bold><hit/> <bold>and you pull 2</bold><destruction/>.
card.effect.5: <bold>Ignore the first</bold> <hit/> <bold>you take</bold> on each of your Rival's turns.
card.effect.6: <bold>Pull 1</bold><fame/> each time you buy a Power card.
card.effect.7: You may roll 1 die for each <hit/> you take. For each <heartFace/> you roll, <bold>ignore 1</bold><hit/>.
card.effect.9: On their next turn, <bold>your Rival rolls 1 less die for each</bold> KEEP card they have. They can decide to discard KEEP cards before resolving this effect.
card.effect.10: <bold>Pull 1</bold><destruction/> for each group of 7<energy/> you have at the end of your turn.
card.effect.13: <bold>Gain 1</bold><energy/> for each die roll you do not use each turn.
card.effect.15: <bold>Pull 1</bold><fame/> if, after resolving your dice, you have no <smashFace/> in your Roll.
card.effect.16: <bold>You may roll the dice 1 additional time</bold> each turn.
card.effect.17: <bold>You do not take</bold> <hit/>. At the start of your turn, you may <bold>discard this card</bold>. If you do not, <bold>gain 6</bold><heart/>, then your turn ends immediately.
card.effect.19: For each <hit/> over 3 that your Rival takes each turn, you may <bold>pay 1</bold><energy/> <bold>to pull 1</bold><fame/>.
card.effect.23: You may <bold>treat all empty spaces on Buzz tokens as</bold> <smash/>.
card.effect.24: <bold>At the start of your turn, you may buy 1 Power card</bold> for 1 less <energy/> (to a minimum of 1).
card.effect.28: When you buy this card, <bold>gain 4</bold><energy/> <bold>and 4</bold><heart/>. <bold>You roll 1 additional die each turn</bold>. If you have at least 4 of a kind in your Roll, <bold>take 10</bold><hit/> before resolving your dice.
card.effect.29: <bold>Pull 1</bold><fame/> for each <smashFace/> you resolved in your Roll this turn.
card.effect.31: You may choose to skip the Resolve Dice Phase. If you do, <bold>gain 2</bold><dice/> <bold>and 2</bold><energy/>, then discard this card.
card.effect.32: Once per turn, when you gain <heart/>, <bold>gain 1 additional</bold><heart/>.
card.effect.34: <bold>Gain 1</bold><dice/> each time you pull the <destruction/> or <fame/> marker off a Buzz token.
card.effect.36: Each time you have 3<powerFace/> in your Roll, <bold>pull 1</bold><fame/>.
card.effect.38: When you pull <destruction/>, <bold>your Rival takes 1</bold><hit/> for each space moved.
card.effect.39: When your Rival gains <energy/> when resolving their dice, you may <bold>discard this card</bold> to <bold>immediately gain an equal number of</bold> <energy/>.
card.effect.40: <bold>Your Rival discards a KEEP card of your choice.</bold> Gain 2<energy/>.
card.effect.41: If a <destruction/> or <fame/> marker is in a <italic>Spotlight Zone</italic>, <bold>the Monster whose side it is on takes 4</bold><hit/>.
card.effect.43: If you pull <destruction/> when resolving your dice, <bold>your Rival rolls 1 less die</bold> on their next turn.
card.effect.44: At the start of the Resolve Dice Phase, you may <bold>choose not to resolve 2 of your dice to gain 1</bold><dice/>.
card.effect.46: If the <fame/> marker is on a Buzz token, <bold>ignore the first</bold> <hit/> <bold>you take</bold> during each of your Rival's turns.
card.effect.47: You may <bold>treat all Buzz bonuses as</bold> <smash/>.
card.effect.48: If you take at least 3<hit/>, you may <bold>exchange this card with on of your Rival's KEEP cards</bold>.
card.effect.49:
When you reach <death/>, <bold>remain at 1</bold><heart/> instead.
<bold>You do not take any more</bold> <hit/> <bold>this turn</bold>. Then, discard this card.
card.effect.50: If you have <fameFace/><destructionFace/><energyFace/><smashFace/><heartFace/><powerFace/> in your Roll, <bold>pull 2</bold><destruction/> <bold>and 2</bold><fame/>, <bold>and your Rival takes 2</bold><hit/>.
tuto.welcome: Welcome to the <bold>King of Tokyo Duel</bold> tutorial!
tuto.goal: In this game, both players embody a Monster and compete for <bold>Fame</bold>, sow <bold>Destruction</bold> and of course, slap each other <smash/>!
tuto.monster: Here's your Monster: you play Gigazaur.
tuto.roll: Now it's your turn: roll the dice by clicking on the button in the header.
tuto.reroll: Each turn you can re-roll all or part of the dice 2 times.
tuto.faces: Each of the 6 faces of the dice is associated with a specific action.
tuto.keep1: Keep your <smashFace/> and <energyFace/> dice and re-roll the others.
tuto.keep2: Keep these dice by clicking on <bold>keep all</bold> in the header.
tuto.use-dices: You can now use the dice you have rolled.
tuto.smash:
The <smashFace/> dice inflict <hit/> on your opponent, who will lose as many hit points <heart/>.
Click <smashFace/> in the header to resolve these dice.
tuto.life: Here's your opponent's life tracker. He has 11 life points left out of the 13 he started with!
tuto.kill: Reducing your opponent's life to 0 is a first step to victory: remember to keep an eye on your life tracker too!
tuto.energy:
The <energyFace/> dice earn <bold>energy cubes</bold> <energy/>.
Click on <energyFace/> in the header to resolve these dice.
tuto.cards:
At the end of your turn, you can use your <bold>energy cubes</bold> <energy/> to buy these <bold>Power cards</bold>.
At any one time, 3 cards are available for purchase.
tuto.cost: The cost of Power cards is shown here. In addition, <bold>the rightmost card costs 1 less</bold> <energy/>.
tuto.pass:
Finally, if you don't buy a card, you earn 1 <energy/>.
Pass in the header to earn 1 <energy/>.
tuto.opponent:
It's your opponent's turn. He'll do the same as you:
• roll the dice up to 3 times
• resolve his dice
• buy cards if he wishes
tuto.turn2:
It's your turn again.
You have 6 dice to roll (there are always 6 dice except in the first round, when the first player rolls only 5 dice).
Roll the dice.
tuto.fame: These dice are used to pull <bold>the Fame marker</bold> <fame/>.
tuto.fame-marker:
When you pull the Fame marker to your side of the board, you gain the
support of the crowds and get closer to victory by becoming the most celebrated Monster.
tuto.keep-fame:
You need to get <bold>3</bold> <fameFace/> dice to move the <fame/> marker one space.
Each additional <fameFace/> adds one space.
Keep your <fameFace/> dice and re-roll the others.
tuto.destruction:
The <destructionFace/> dice work like the <fameFace/> dice but allow you to draw <bold>the Destruction marker</bold> <destruction/>.
When you pull the Destruction marker on your side of the board, you ravage the city and get closer to victory by becoming the most feared Monster.
tuto.keep-fame2: Keep your <fameFace/> die and re-roll the other 2.
tuto.heal:
The <healFace/> dice allow you to recover lost <heart/>. Resolve them.
<italic>You can't exceed your</italic> <heart/> <italic>starting value</italic>.
tuto.pull-fame:
Now solve your <fameFace/> dice.
A triple allows you to move the marker 1 space, and each additional die adds 1 space: so you'll move <fame/> 2 spaces.
tuto.marker-win: If you draw the Fame <fame/> or Destruction <destruction/> marker at the end of the track, <bold>you win the game</bold>!
tuto.spotlight: You can also win by pulling the Fame marker <fame/> AND the Destruction marker <destruction/> <bold>under the spotlight</bold> to your side, in this area.
tuto.power:
The <powerFace/> dice have a different effect for each Monster.
At this game you play <bold>Gigazaur</bold>. For 2 <powerFace/>, he can pull <fame/> or <destruction/> 1 time.
So he's strong to win with Fame or Destruction!
tuto.buy:
You can now buy <bold>Power cards</bold>.
You have enough <energy/> to buy this one, thanks to its discount.
Buy it.
tuto.card-type:
There are 2 types of card: DISCARD cards have an immediate effect and are then discarded.
KEEP cards are retained, their effect lasting as long as the player has them in front of them.
tuto.buzz: Some power cards have a symbol drawn here below their cost. When you buy this card, <bold>you place the corresponding Buzz token on the board</bold>.
tuto.buzz-bonus:
Buzz tokens give bonuses that players get when they pull the <fame/> or <destruction/> markers on them.
Place this buzz token on the Destruction track, immediately to the left of the <destruction/> marker.
tuto.card-effect: Once the buzz token has been placed, the effect of the card is applied. In this case, you pull <fame/> and <destruction/> 1 time each.
tuto.dice-token:
By pulling the <destruction/> token on the buzz token you've just placed, you earn the corresponding bonus.
Here it is: an <bold>extra dice token</bold>.
tuto.extra-dice:
At the start of your turn, you can spend an <bold>extra dice token</bold> to add one of the 2 white dice to your roll this turn.
Please note, you can never have more than 2 <bold>extra dice tokens</bold>, so remember to use them!
tuto.win:
Now it's up to you.
<bold>Remember, to win, you can:</bold>
• reduce to 0 <heart/> the life of your opponent
• pull <fame/> OR <destruction/> to the end of the track
• pull <fame/> AND <destruction/> under the spotlight
tuto.end:
At any time, click on the material for a reminder of the associated rules.
Click "Pass" in the header to complete your turn, and good luck!
